title: Procurement Approval Process Guidelines
content:
# Procurement Approval Process

## Approval Authority
- Under 500,000: Department Manager approval
- 500,000 to 1,000,000: Director approval
- Over 1,000,000: CEO approval

## Vendor Evaluation Criteria (Weighted Scoring)
- Price competitiveness (30%)
- Delivery capability (25%)
- Product quality (20%)
- Partnership relationship (15%)
- After-sales support (10%)

## Important Notes
- Single-supplier procurement must not exceed 60% of total amount
- First-time procurement from new suppliers is capped at 1,000,000
- VIP suppliers are eligible for the expedited approval process
- Additional justification is required when procurement amount exceeds 80% of total budget
- All vendor evaluations must be documented and archived for review

title: Server Selection Guide
content:
# Server Selection Guide

## Selection Principles
1. Business requirements alignment: Choose configurations matching workload types
2. Compatibility verification: Confirm compatibility with existing infrastructure
3. Scalability assessment: Reserve expansion capacity for the next 3 years
4. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO): Consider full lifecycle costs including purchase, operations, and power consumption

## Brand Comparison Reference
- Dell PowerEdge: Mature ecosystem, abundant accessories, suitable for hybrid environments
- HP ProLiant: Comprehensive management tools, excellent enterprise-grade stability
- Huawei FusionServer: Xinchuang compliant, top choice for domestic technology substitution
- Lenovo ThinkSystem: Strong value for money, reliability certified internationally

## Important Notes
- Xinchuang compliance projects should prioritize domestic brands
- Ensure unified management platforms when deploying mixed brands
- Mission-critical systems should select brands with local after-sales support
